title: Daily Market Digest: May 25, 2026
subtitle: Global Optimism on Iran Deal, U.S. Futures Point Higher Ahead of Holiday-Shortened Week

items.headline: Global Markets Rally on Iran Deal Hopes; U.S. Futures Point Higher Despite Holiday Closure
items.summary: Global markets are experiencing a significant uplift today, May 25, 2026, driven by optimism over a potential US-Iran peace deal. This sentiment is easing geopolitical tensions and pushing oil prices lower. Despite U.S. cash markets being closed for Memorial Day, futures for major indices are signaling a positive open for the shortened trading week, building on recent record highs.

items.statContext: Reflecting positive sentiment despite Memorial Day closure
Tech-heavy index buoyed by AI optimism

items.headline: Nvidia Continues AI-Driven Ascent Post Strong Earnings
items.summary: Shares of semiconductor giant Nvidia are showing continued strength, building on its impressive Q1 FY27 earnings report which saw revenue surge 85% year-over-year, driven by robust AI infrastructure demand.

items.headline: enGene Therapeutics Plunges on Updated Interim Results
items.summary: enGene Therapeutics Inc. ($ENGN) saw a significant decline, with shares down over 80% for the month, following the announcement of updated interim results from its LEGEND pivotal cohort.

items.headline: Optimism Grows for US-Iran Deal, Pushing Oil Prices Down
items.summary: Hopes for a potential US-Iran interim agreement are bolstering global markets and sending crude oil prices lower, with Brent crude falling below $100 per barrel. President Trump noted negotiations are 'largely negotiated,' aiming to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: Fed Minutes Signal Potential Shift to Restrictive Stance, Rate Hike Odds Increase
items.summary: Minutes from the April FOMC meeting reveal a potential shift away from an easing bias, with many participants favoring removing language suggesting future rate cuts. With April PCE inflation data expected later this week, odds of a Fed rate hike in 2026 have surged, following New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh taking office.

items.headline: Energy and Financials Gain Momentum as Tech Shows Signs of Weakening in Sector Rotation
items.summary: A significant 'Growth-to-Value' rotation is observed, with Energy and Financial sectors showing strong momentum. While the AI infrastructure boom continues to support companies like Nvidia, the Information Technology sector is showing signs of weakening in relative momentum, raising concerns about AI concentration.

items.headline: Key Economic Data and Major Earnings Reports Await Shortened Trading Week
items.summary: Following the Memorial Day holiday, investors anticipate key economic data releases this week, including April PCE inflation and the second estimate of Q1 GDP on Thursday. Major companies like Salesforce ($CRM), Dell ($DELL), and Costco ($COST) are also scheduled to report earnings, providing further insights into corporate health and consumer spending.
